text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Fiona  
Centre for Epilepsy  
Queen Square, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Fiona,                                                                                                         

Daniel Harris, DOB: 22-11-1990, Hospital No: H384920 NHS No. 9134756201  
Flat 5, 28 Redchurch Street, London E2 7DP  

Focal Epilepsy 

Medication: Lamotrigine titrated since August 2025  

Current dose: Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily (patient-reported adherence without missed doses in the last month) 

Lamotrigine Blood Level:  

15 September 2025: 4.2 mg/L (laboratory reference 1.0–15.0 mg/L). Dose at sampling: 100 mg twice daily.  

Seizures: Patient reports focal aware sensory episodes progressing to focal impaired awareness seizures only when significantly short on sleep; outside of nights with curtailed rest, no events have occurred in the past eight weeks. The last event was on 10 September 2025 after an overnight shift and early start the following day. No generalised tonic–clonic seizures reported.  

History: Prior history of migraine without aura since adolescence, currently infrequent and managed with simple analgesia. No recent head injury or intercurrent illness. No alcohol excess; no recreational drug use. Patient identifies irregular sleep and missing meals as potential seizure precipitants.  

Assessment: Clinical picture is consistent with focal epilepsy. Semiology begins with tingling in the right hand and epigastric rising sensation, followed by brief speech arrest and unresponsiveness lasting 1–2 minutes, with quick recovery and no injury. Neurological examination previously normal. MRI brain (July 2025) unremarkable. EEG (August 2025) showed intermittent left temporal sharp activity.  

Plan: Continue Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily. Reinforce sleep hygiene and regular meal routine. Patient has been provided with written safety advice regarding bathing/swimming, heights, and operating machinery. Driving status discussed in line with DVLA guidance: patient aware to inform DVLA and to follow seizure-related restrictions. We will repeat a trough Lamotrigine level in four weeks and review in clinic thereafter. Patient to contact the service sooner if seizures occur despite adequate sleep or if there are medication side effects (rash, dizziness, diplopia).  

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Patient reports focal aware sensory episodes progressing to focal impaired awareness seizures only when significantly short on sleep; outside of nights with curtailed rest, no events have occurred in the past eight weeks. The last event was on 10 September 2025

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter states the patient has focal aware sensory episodes progressing to focal impaired awareness seizures only when significantly short on sleep. It also says that outside of nights with curtailed rest, no events have occurred in the past eight weeks, and the last event was on 10 September 2025. The clinic date is 02 October 2025, so the last seizure was about 3 weeks prior. The letter does not provide a numeric rate (per day/week/month/year) over a defined period; it only specifies that events occur under a particular trigger and that there have been no events in the past eight weeks except the one on 10 September. This is insufficient to calculate a clear frequency, and the patient is not seizure free for 6 months. Therefore, the frequency is unknown.
seizure_frequency_number: Patient reports focal aware sensory episodes progressing to focal impaired awareness seizures only when significantly short on sleep; outside of nights with curtailed rest, no events have occurred in the past eight weeks. The last event was on 10 September 2025
